<!-- auto-generated by docgen — do not hand-edit. Run `tools/docgen/docgen` to refresh. -->
<!-- docgen:version=0.1.0 reason: first generation; single-file hook directory -->

## Purpose
Holds the git hook(s) that make docgen self-maintaining: this is where the `pre-push` hook lives that auto-refreshes the generated READMEs in the background after every user push, so docs never drift from code.

## Files
- `pre-push` — fires before each `git push`; immediately exits 0 (never blocks the user), but first self-spawns a detached background worker (via `setsid -f`, falling back to `nohup &`) that waits 5s, runs `tools/docgen/docgen --until-done --parallel 4`, and if any READMEs changed, commits them (`--no-verify`) and pushes the refresh back to the same branch. If that push fails because the branch was merged + deleted, it opens a fresh follow-up PR off `main` via `gh pr create`. Carries the `DOCGEN_PRE_PUSH_HOOK_v1` marker so `install-push-hook.sh` can recognize it as ours.

## Dependencies / collaborators
- `tools/docgen/docgen` — the CLI the background worker invokes to regenerate READMEs.
- `tools/docgen/install-push-hook.sh` — installs this file into `.git/hooks/` and uses the `DOCGEN_PRE_PUSH_HOOK_v1` marker to avoid clobbering a foreign hook.
- `.docgen/state.json` + `**/README.md` — the artifacts the worker stages and commits.
- `gh` CLI — only used on the merged-branch fallback path to open a follow-up PR.

## Gotchas
- **Loop prevention is env-var-based.** The background worker sets `DOCGEN_HOOK_SKIP=1` before its own push; the hook noops at the top when that's set. Two more internal vars drive control flow: `DOCGEN_HOOK_REFRESH_NOW=1` marks the detached re-invocation (the same script doing the background work), and `DOCGEN_HOOK_REMOTE` carries the remote through. Don't strip these or you'll either get an infinite push loop or a no-op.
- **All background output goes to `/tmp/docgen-push-hook.log`** — the foreground push shows nothing, so that log is the only place to debug why a refresh didn't happen.
- **The hook drains stdin (`cat > /dev/null`)** because pre-push hooks block until the pushed-refs stream is consumed; the refs themselves are currently unused.
- **Background failures always `exit 0`** by design — a broken docgen run silently won't refresh docs rather than break your push; absence of a refresh commit is the only symptom.
